metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Agreeable Agreeableness (74th pct) — Cooperative, trusting, empathetic
Conscientious Conscientiousness (68th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Highly extraverted Extraversion (84th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (97th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly conventional Openness to Experience (19th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  High Tender-Mindedness (76th pct) — Sympathetic, moved by others' needs
  High Achievement Striving (60th pct) — Ambitious, driven, works hard toward goals
  High Self-Discipline (70th pct) — Self-disciplined, persistent, finishes tasks
  High Activity Level (76th pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  Very High Excitement Seeking (86th pct) — Thrill-seeking, excitement-hungry
  Very High Positive Emotions (85th pct) — Joyful, enthusiastic, optimistic
  Very High Anger / Hostility (100th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (100th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (99th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (43rd pct)

message.reasoning:
We need to simulate a baseline opinion on "Trust_In_Mainstream_Journalism". The profile is a 43-year-old female with very high neuroticism (angry, anxious, self-conscious, impulsive), very high extraversion (assertive, excitement-seeking, warm), high agreeableness (compliant, tender-minded, trusting at 60th pct which is moderate), high conscientiousness (dutiful, competent), and very low openness (conventional, practical). 

Key traits: high neuroticism -> prone to suspicion, anxiety, distrust; low openness -> prefers tradition, familiar sources; high extraversion -> outgoing but assertive; high agreeableness -> trusting (60th pct) and compliant, but tender-minded. Her trust facet is at 60th pct, which is moderate, not extremely high. However, her openness is very low (19th pct), suggesting she may not embrace new or alternative information sources, possibly relying on mainstream? But neuroticism often correlates with distrust and belief in conspiracy. Research shows neuroticism is associated with lower trust in institutions and media. Extraversion sometimes links to more trust? Mixed. Given her high anger/hostility, anxiety, she may be skeptical of mainstream journalism. Low openness suggests traditionalism, but she might be wary of elites. 

Also consider her age: 43, not elderly. 

Overall, I expect a moderate negative leaning. Not extreme because she has moderate trust facet. But neuroticism is high (97th pct), which pulls down trust. Compliance is high (90th pct) suggests deference to authority? But "compliance" in Big Five refers to tendency to cooperate with others, not necessarily trust institutions. 

I'll produce a score around -300 to -500, maybe -412. Confidence moderate. Reasoning: high neuroticism and low openness combined with high anger-hostility create skepticism, but moderate trust facet and dutifulness temper extreme distrust. So mild opposition.
